transcript: Good afternoon everyone. My name is George Bousis and I'm the founder and CEO at Raise. I think it's really important to start with a little backstory. We first started... this is a culmination of a decade of work and we first started... ultimately trying to solve how blockchains could solve a lot of the issues that we were running into in the gift card space in and around fraud. That quickly evolved into us launching the first tokenized gift card in 2015 on Ethereum. We thought we had it all figured out and that the retailers would love the product. But when we tried to commercialize it, we realized that Ethereum was just too slow and too expensive for this to work. So where a lot of other startups quit, we put our heads down and tried to figure this out. We spent the next decade trying to solve this issue. And what I'm most excited about today is I think we finally cracked it. And that's why we're going big today with Solana to launch SmartCards. For those of you that are wondering who Raise is... we've been around for over 13 years and we're a leader in the global payments and gift card space. We've raised over $265 million from some of the biggest names in venture. Blue chip firms such as NEA, Accel, PayPal, Haun, and many others. We've done over $5 billion in gift card sales to date, saving our customers hundreds of millions of dollars. And we have over 7 million active customers in our business and reach 80 million people in our B2B business. We have the Raise Cashback App which you can download today in the App Store. You can earn cashback by spending fiat or crypto at millions of stores. We also have a B2B business called Raise for Business. We power the likes of Bilt Rewards, Citibank, and many others. We also have the Raise Network, which is our blockchain-powered gift cards that we're talking about today. And our foundation is the Retail Alliance which promotes innovation in the gift card industry. For those of you that aren't familiar, the gift card space is massive. I hear a lot of folks talking about the next billion users. The reality is they're here. This year more than a billion people will purchase $1.2 trillion in gift cards. And that number is going to 2x over the next five years to more than $2.3 trillion by 2030. But the industry really hasn't evolved in over 100 years since the first paper certificates were created. We've merely gone from paper certificates to plastic cards to email, all which are pretty non-secure ways to send and receive money. And it's because everyone wants a piece. You have issuers, processors, distributors, activators, card printers, everyone trying to take a piece of the pie. And retailers are stuck having to spend as much as 20% of the value of a gift card in fees. So if we take back $1.2 trillion this year, retailers will pay a quarter trillion dollars in fees. We think we can solve this. Because today they're relying on breakage, yield, and overspend just to break even. And this turns gift cards into a financial offset rather than a customer benefit. Which is why we're reinventing gift cards. Blockchains will transform the multi-trillion dollar gift card industry. It's going to help solve a lot of these technical limitations that exist while unlocking new growth opportunities and initiatives for retailers. Because we believe gift cards are one of retail's most overlooked assets. Raise turns them into fraud-proof, brand-owned instruments that convert everyday purchases into more measurable and deeper engagement and measurable loyalty, all at a fraction of the price. Because this is where gift cards break down today: fraud and theft, breakage abuse, security vulnerabilities, and future quantum threats. Now, as we mentioned, we aren't new to this. We've published a lot of content that we're going to be sharing with all of you on our site. This has been more than 10 years in the making. We have dozens of issued patents in the space. I think a lot of people thought we were crazy when we were doing this, which we had to be a little. But I'm sure the big question a lot of you are asking are what are SmartCards? SmartCards are a new class of digital assets designed for speed, scale, and configurability. They're programmatic by design. So you have inputs and outputs that can be programmed directly into the smart contract, such as weather, scores, events, outcomes, data. And the result of that is smart contract logic for retailers. So when we're working with brands like AMC Theatres and it happens to rain in a city like Miami where I'm from, AMC Theatres can programmatically offer 50% discounts to the theaters to get people to go to the movies. Why? Because they're still paying their employees, they're still screening the movies, they're still making popcorn. The weather is pretty unpredictable. And so SmartCards solve today's biggest challenges around data, cost savings, robust security, flexible utility, personalized offers, and transferability, just to name a few. And they also improve operations too. There's fast settlement, low issuance costs, and cheaper distribution. And the good news is we're live. This isn't just a vision anymore. We launched in Mainnet just a couple weeks ago and have already issued 50,000 cards on-chain. That number will scale to millions as we continue to bring more retailers into our platform. Brands such as Best Buy or Darden Restaurant Group or CVS, just to name a few. And so what's next? We're building on Solana with strategic support from the Foundation, which we're really thankful for. We're live on Mainnet and deployed to the Raise Network. Raise One is live with a series of new product launches coming in 2026 that we're really excited to share. And there's a token that's coming in the first half of next year. So we'd love for you to build the future of gift cards and loyalty with us. You can find more information here by scanning the QR code or looking us up on X or just go to raise.com or raise.network. Thank you guys so much.

# Solflare unveils self-custody crypto debit card
Today, Solflare, the gateway to the next era of finance, has partnered with Mastercard, to launch the Solflare Card, the first true self-custody debit card on Solana.
[]()019 November 2025[Be the first to comment](#comments)
#### External
This content is provided by an external author without editing by Finextra. It expresses the views and opinions of the author.
Seamlessly linking to users' Solflare Wallets, the new digital card allows users to make instant debit card payments with USDC while maintaining full control of their assets, without preloading or prior conversion. The Solflare card will initially be available to those in the UK and European Economic Area (EEA), before being rolled out in additional regions.
Featuring built-in biometric verification, fraud detection mechanism, real-time notifications and 3DS, the Solflare Card is designed to enable users to spend crypto as fiat, instantly, securely, and globally. By partnering with Mastercard, the Solflare Card will enable seamless stablecoin and crypto transactions at more than 150 million Mastercard-accepting merchants worldwide.
“Crypto is no longer simply a speculative asset class, and we want our 4 million+ strong community to use crypto in whatever way suits their daily needs”, said Filip Dragoslavic, Co-Founder and Co-CEO of Solflare. “With the Solflare Card, we are making it faster, safer, and easier to participate in the crypto economy. Crypto spending shouldn’t feel risky or overly complicated. The Solflare Card brings control back to users, with security and simplicity built-in.”
Christian Rau, senior vice president of Global Partnerships, Digital Assets, Blockchain and Fintech Enablement at Mastercard, said: "Mastercard is committed to integrating digital currencies into the broader payments ecosystem. By working with partners like Solflare, we further that mission and empower users through safe, simple and instant access to their crypto assets – truly bridging the gap between digital asset ecosystem and everyday payments.”
The Solflare Card is linked to users’ non-custodial Solflare wallet, meaning users maintain control of their private keys while Solflare manages the rails. This keeps users’ private keys secure, never exposed or transferred, while each transaction is signed directly (with the user’s approval) on the card, supported by three-factor authentication, including biometric verification, a PIN, and the physical card itself. Upon launch, Solflare cards are compatible with Google Pay, with Apple Pay coming soon, for fast, highly secure, contactless payments.
Vidor Gencel, Co-Founder and Co-CEO of Solflare, added, ““With over 115K of our loyal community members signed up for the

# Powering the next wave of digital asset utility with cards
By Ariana Poursartip
December 2, 2025
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/66c78bffc753432b33c4f644/6927b4c5df98221716ec758f_Partnership%20post.jpg)
With Bridge's global card issuing platform[launch](https://usa.visa.com/about-visa/newsroom/press-releases.releaseId.21371.html)earlier this year, the barrier between holding stablecoins and spending them has disappeared. Developers can now offer their users the ability to pay at millions of merchants worldwide directly from their stablecoin balances, without friction, conversion delays, or the need to offramp to traditional banking.
‍For developers building on Bridge, card programs unlock new opportunities to enhance customer loyalty, boost engagement, and generate additional revenue through card interchange. The launch also compliments Stripe’s overall issuing capabilities, extending stablecoin-backed card programs for business audiences to consumer applications.
‍Since the launch, Bridge has been heads down listening closely to developers, expanding our offering, and helping customers launch in new markets. Today we’re thrilled to share a new wave of customers launching innovative spending solutions alongside new card issuing functionality.
‍## Innovators bringing more utility to stablecoins with card spend
‍From crypto wallets and remittance providers to neobanks, businesses are empowering their users with seamless ways to spend their digital assets.
‍Today Zepz, the global payments group behind WorldRemit and Sendwave serving 9 million customers, announced that Sendwave customers will soon be able to spend their stablecoin balances on everyday purchases through a Bridge-powered card. In October this year, Zepz announced the[Sendwave Wallet](https://zepz.io/press/zepz-launches-sendwave-wallet-to-give-customers-the-power-of-stablecoins-in-everyday-transactions/), enabling users to send, store, and spend USDC via a stablecoin-backed peer-to-peer cross border money solution supporting customers across 100 countries. Using Bridge’s smart contract functionality that links cards with their wallet infrastructure, Zepz can empower customers in multiple countries to compliantly spend stablecoin balances through one integration.
‍The card product, expected to go live in some markets including Brazil in early 2026, builds on Zepz’s mission to make financial empowerment simple, trusted and accessible for cross-border communities worldwide.
‍“For years, cross-border communities have been underserved by a financial system that wasn’t built for them. We’re changing that. By leveraging Bridge's infrastructure, Sendwave customers can use their digital dollar balance to not just send, receive and hold funds but also to spend them as an integral part of their daily lives. This isn’t just about moving money, but giving people better access, more stability and financial choice in their daily lives through a simple and trusted experience.”
Mark Lenhard, CEO of Zepz
Zepz
‍Phantom, a crypto wallet with more than 15 million users, is removing the friction around daily usage of crypto with[Phantom Cash](https://phantom.com/cash). Users can top up their balance through various funding methods, pay friends, and soon spend worldwide using a Bridge-powered card. Balances can be held and spent in CASH, a new stablecoin designed by Phantom and built on Bridge’s Open Issuance platform.h various funding methods, pay friends, and soon spend worldwide using a Bridge-powered card. Balances can be held and spent in CASH, a new stablecoin designed by Phantom and built on Bridge’s Open Issuance platform.

tags.impact_reason: Raise SmartCards on Solana could streamline gift card transactions and reduce fees for retailers, offering a tangible benefit to consumers, but the actual adoption rate remains to be seen.

The visual narrative transforms a familiar plastic card into a high-tech, semi-translucent artifact with glowing circuitry, representing its migration from a legacy system to the advanced Solana blockchain. I included the text '$5B' laser-etched onto the card's surface to diegetically anchor the massive scale of Raise's gift card empire, making the abstract financial figure a tangible part of the new technology.

Its interior reveals intricate, glowing circuitry in Solana's green and purple hues. The card's surface is a mix of brushed titanium and clear polycarbonate, with the text '$5B' precisely laser-etched into the metal, catching the internal light. The background is a deep, out-of-focus server room, with subtle data streams suggesting the massive scale of the migration.

Precisely laser-etched into the brushed titanium surface, the '$5B' text glows with an internal light, catching the intricate reflections and adding to the card's technological artifact aesthetic. The SmartCard's vertical orientation emphasizes immense height and epic scale, with significant negative space stretching upwards into the dark expanse. In the deeply out-of-focus background, a vast server room is subtly visible, its bokeh-laden lights and faint, ephemeral data streams subtly suggesting the massive scale of a data migration. editorial.headline: Raise Migrates $5B Gift Card Empire to Solana Mainnet
editorial.standfirst: After a failed Ethereum pilot in 2015, the payments giant is moving its 7 million users to Solana. The pivot introduces 'SmartCards'—programmatic assets that allow retailers like AMC to automate dynamic, on-chain pricing.
key_takeaways: 50,000 SmartCards are already live on Solana Mainnet, with plans to scale to millions for brands like Best Buy and CVS.
Raise brings institutional scale: $265M in backing (NEA, Accel, PayPal) and $5B in historical transaction volume.
A native network token is confirmed for launch in H1 2026.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
For George Bousis, the path to Solana was paved by a decade-old failure. In 2015, his company **Raise** launched the first tokenized gift card on Ethereum, only to hit a wall of high gas fees and slow finality that made the unit economics impossible for low-value retail transactions. Ten years later, the payments infrastructure giant—which has processed over $5 billion in volume—has found its solution. At Breakpoint 2025, Bousis announced that Raise has officially migrated its infrastructure to Solana, with 50,000 **SmartCards** already minted on Mainnet.

The implications for the $1.2 trillion gift card industry go beyond simple tokenization. Raise is replacing static digital receipts with programmable assets. During the keynote, Bousis detailed how this logic transforms retail operations: a brand like **AMC Theatres** can now program SmartCards to automatically trigger 50% discounts in specific regions based on real-time weather data oracle feeds. This shifts gift cards from passive liabilities into active, dynamic marketing channels that drive foot traffic when businesses need it most—all while eliminating the 20% fees retailers currently pay to legacy intermediaries.

This is not a testnet experiment. **Raise** is live today, backed by $265 million from heavyweights like **NEA**, **Accel**, **PayPal Ventures**, and **Haun Ventures**. The platform serves 7 million active customers and powers B2B solutions for **Citibank** and **Bilt Rewards**. By moving this volume on-chain, Raise is effectively bringing a massive, non-speculative user base to the Solana network, validating the chain's capacity to handle high-frequency, real-world commerce.

Looking ahead, the roadmap is aggressive. The company is rolling out its "Raise One" platform and has confirmed a native token launch for the first half of 2026. For institutional investors, this signals a rare opportunity: a Web3 infrastructure play that arrives with a decade of Web2 revenue, deep retailer partnerships (including **Darden Restaurant Group** and **Best Buy**), and a clear utility model that solves a quarter-trillion-dollar efficiency problem for merchants.
